item.dc:creator: Miiaka

item.title: Still an important message.
item.description: I know, this is a repost, but I haven't seen it circulate for a while, and it's very important. As someone of Jewish descent, this really hits home for me. I don't have family members who were in the camps, but the stories were still told in my household. When I was in middle school we had to read a holocaust book and had the author (a holocaust survivor) speak about his experience in an assembly and for me it was a similar experience to what the author of these tweets described. A lot of the students were clearly blindsided by the intensity of the stories, and it was hard to listen to, even knowing about it already. I'm not a violent person, but I can make an exception for nazis. (originally posted by @truemetalman on 2021-01-29 22:42:17)

item.title: Nobody tell Republicans about the Streisand Effect.
item.description: Gonna publish a controversial book, just to get it banned from schools, for the free publicity. The irony of Maus becoming a best seller because they wanted to keep people from reading it, is just...*chef's kiss*

item.title: If you molest a child in Texas, you might just get beaten to death.
item.description:
This is a big local story, right now - there's currently a very popular petition going around, to release all 3 of the boys involved in this case, because many consider the homicide, justifiable. I agree.
What this particular article fails to mention, is that Quintanilla's warrant for being a serial child predator, wasn't exactly being treated as a "high priority", by Pharr police. They describe him as 'elusively evading arrest', for 2 FUCKING YEARS - cleverly hiding in plain sight at the house where he lived with his family. Had police done their jobs, he might not have ever victimized his own daughter, leading that little girl's brothers to take the law into their own hands. He'd be alive and in jail, if the justice system had worked the way it's supposed to.
I honestly don't see prosecutors being able to secure a conviction, in this case. It has "jury nullification" written all over it. The general consensus in the public is that these kids did society a favor, and that their actions are not only understandable, but commendable. The prosecution is wasting their time, IMO - vigilante justice is tolerated here, when child sex abuse is the catalyst. It's not unprecedented, either. About a decade ago, an extremely similar situation took place in another Texas town:
In that case, the murdered man was NOT currently wanted for prior child-rape (again - for TWO. FUCKING. YEARS.), and if I remember correctly, he was never even arrested for it. The grand jury was essentially a formality. I don't blame the defendants in either case, personally. If I found out someone did that to my daughter (or little sister), I'd fucking snap. I hope these guys have a good attorney. Jailing them feels like an injustice, here. Links: https://www.mysanantonio.com/news/local/article/Texas-brothers-beat-stepfather-to-death-16805544.php https://abcnews.go.com/US/charges-texas-father-beat-death-daughters-molester/story?id=16612071
item.dc:creator: ButtersTheCat

Good people.
Takes a shot of salt instead of alcohol.
Got ‘em

It says, “thank you” in Russian.
Cat tax I hope you all enjoyed the dump. Have a great rest of your day!

item.title: Have some memes.
item.description: Slowly coming to terms with the fact that my relationship is ending. I'm straight up not having a good time rn. So here are some assorted memes to take my mind of things. Enjoy Fp edit: thank you all for your kind words, and all of the good advice. I am truly grateful, and I love you all.

item.title: Please do right by your animals
item.description:
This is Bubba. Bubba was brought in to see me by his owners concerned friend. This poor guy had years worth of Mats, and permanently damaged feet do to neglect.
His legs were matted to his body, making movements painful, and he was rather aggressive during the first part of our shave down. After cutting out a few mats, and giving him lots of love, he seemed to understand that I meant to help him instead of hurt him.
This poor guy was in desperate need of a nail trim. When nails get to a certain length, they can change the tendon and bone structure in a dogs feet, making it uncomfortable to walk, and can cause permanent damage. These nails caused Bubba to limp around using only the outside of his paw pads.
The mats on Bubbas face came into his mouth, wrapping around some of his teeth. He was always chewing on hair.
I am beyond grateful for the wonderful woman who brought Bubba in to see me. She asked me to come in on my birthday to make him feel better, and I am so glad she did! He is not her dog, but she knew he needed help.
Please do not neglect your animals. They love you unconditionally, and deserve to be treated well. Be good to them, be good to each other.
item.dc:creator: RaptorAttack007
